rawText: Sunday felt pretty steady. I slept close to seven hours, made breakfast, and kept my usual rhythm through the day.
Work at Northlight Studio was manageable today. Nia called me around lunch from 602-555-0139, and that quick check-in helped.
I got tense after lunch near Tempe Marketplace and stayed jumpy for a while. I settled down by evening, but it took effort.
The morning walk at Kiwanis Park helped clear my head. Sleep was still okay, and I sent a check-in note to coach@quietpath.org.
My routine slipped tonight. I stayed up too late reading Valley Recovery Clinic paperwork (MRN-8842), and the whole next day felt rushed.
I only got about five hours of sleep. My head felt noisy, I skipped lunch, and I kept replaying what Dr. Hannah Lee said.
Barely slept and felt wired most of the day. I almost called an old contact, Ray M., at 480-555-7781, then put my phone down.
I stayed home at 1120 E Alameda Dr and ignored a couple invites. I felt isolated, irritable, and skipped dinner with friends again.
Another rough night. I skipped my evening routine, felt shaky, and left Maya Rivera's messages unread.
After work I had a strong urge to use again. I texted my sponsor, Luis, and emailed luis.r@harbormail.com instead of acting on it.
Cravings got louder around 6:45 PM in the Banner Baywood parking lot, and my focus dropped off hard.
Still tense tonight, but I paused and took a short walk before bed. I also called Dr. Patel at Mesa Counseling Center to set a follow-up.
I talked with Luis for about ten minutes and was honest about how the week went. We set a follow-up for 03/13/2026.
I want to bring a summary of this week to Dr. Lee at Valley Clinic, but I still want my phone number and home address kept private.
